name: Alexinof
epithet: The One Arm That Was Enough
bio: Alexinof cut off his own arm when it was infected. This is the fact that most efficiently describes him, not because it is the most important thing about him but because it is the thing that tells you everything about how he makes decisions. He had the infection, he had a blade, he had the assessment that one outcome was preferable to the other, and the assessment took a fraction of a second. He has no room for hesitation. He removed hesitation as a possibility and has operated without it since.

He was Russian special forces, left behind in the same sweep that left Seira. He had been studying Vornakof's movements for two weeks by the time the crew found him — not because he had orders, but because he had identified Vornakof as the thing that needed ending and was waiting until the ending could be done efficiently rather than merely done. He joined the crew with the same economy he applied to everything else.

He takes the center approach on the cathedral assault, which nobody else had assessed as viable. He is correct that it is viable. The correctness costs him something that he does not mention. He leaves the crew at the first stop after extraction because he operates alone and has for long enough that it is structural rather than preferential. He will go where he is useful. He will not announce it.
